response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which are present in some aquatic environments but not in all underwater locations however even if they avoided peanut exposure underwater their bodies would still require oxygen which is not available in the same form as on land and their respiratory system is not adapted to extract oxygen from water Sec. 2  
furthermore living underwater would require a completely different set of physiological adaptations which humans do not possess such as gills or the ability to hold their breath for extended periods additionally the presence of other allergens in underwater environments could complicate their condition further making it even more dangerous for them to reside in such conditions
